meta.project: GymCoach Studio — deterministic fitness knowledge base

status_legend.DATA_COMPLETE: Real generated dataset exists below in `data` field. Ready to wire into the app.
status_legend.DATA_PENDING: Schema/spec only for now (`spec_text`). This is what MASTER_PROMPT targets next.
status_legend.LOGIC_ONLY: Pure decision logic / formulas / infra policy. No per-exercise dataset needed — gets implemented directly as Python from `spec_text`. Do NOT send these to the data-generation AI.

meta.movement_taxonomy_note: Engine 1's original spec lists 11 canonical movement_ids. The actual generated dataset (Engine 2, 401 exercises) uses an extended set of 15 — this is now the real canonical list, see engine 1 below. All DATA_PENDING engines must use these same 15 movement_ids.

1.name: MOVEMENT INTELLIGENCE
1.spec_text:
## Purpose
Canonical source of truth for movement classification.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: movement_intelligence
primary_key: movement_id
references:
  exercise_metadata: exercise_id
```

## Canonical Movement IDs
| movement_id | Category | Plane | Chain |
|---|---|---|---|
| squat | Lower Body | Sagittal | Closed |
| hip_hinge | Lower Body | Sagittal | Closed |
| horizontal_push | Upper Body | Sagittal | Mixed |
| vertical_push | Upper Body | Frontal | Mixed |
| horizontal_pull | Upper Body | Sagittal | Mixed |
| vertical_pull | Upper Body | Frontal | Mixed |
| lunge | Lower Body | Sagittal | Closed |
| carry | Full Body | Multi | Closed |
| rotation | Core | Transverse | Mixed |
| anti_rotation | Core | Transverse | Mixed |
| gait | Locomotion | Multi | Closed |

### Deterministic Rules
1. One primary movement_id per exercise.
2. Secondary patterns optional.
3. Movement taxonomy cannot be redefined downstream.
4. Exercise Metadata SHALL reference movement_id.

2.name: EXERCISE METADATA
2.spec_text:
## Purpose
Canonical database describing every exercise.

### Schema
```yaml
exercise_id: string
display_name: string
movement_id: string
primary_muscles: list
secondary_muscles: list
stabilizers: list
equipment: list
difficulty: beginner|intermediate|advanced
exercise_type: compound|isolation
joint_stress_profile: string
fatigue_profile: string
progressions: list
regressions: list
substitutions: list
```

## Canonical Equipment
- barbell
- dumbbell
- kettlebell
- cable
- machine
- smith_machine
- resistance_band
- bodyweight
- suspension_trainer

## Canonical Muscle Taxonomy
Primary groups:
- chest
- back
- shoulders
- quadriceps
- hamstrings
- glutes
- calves
- biceps
- triceps
- forearms
- core

## Sample Exercise Records

### barbell_back_squat
```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
display_name: Barbell Back Squat
primary_muscles:
- quadriceps
- glutes
secondary_muscles:
- hamstrings
- adductors
stabilizers:
- erector_spinae
- abdominals
equipment:
- barbell
difficulty: intermediate
exercise_type: compound
joint_stress_profile: JST_SQUAT_001
fatigue_profile: STF_SQUAT_001
progressions:
- paused_back_squat
- front_squat
regressions:
- goblet_squat
- box_squat
substitutions:
- hack_squat
- belt_squat
```

### dumbbell_bench_press
```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
display_name: Dumbbell Bench Press
primary_muscles:
- chest
secondary_muscles:
- triceps
- anterior_deltoid
equipment:
- dumbbell
difficulty: beginner
exercise_type: compound
joint_stress_profile: JST_HPUSH_001
fatigue_profile: STF_HPUSH_001
progressions:
- incline_db_press
regressions:
- machine_chest_press
substitutions:
- barbell_bench_press
```

## Validation
- exercise_id must be globally unique.
- Every exercise references one movement_id.
- Equipment names must use canonical taxonomy.
- Muscle names must use canonical taxonomy.
- Downstream engines reference exercise_id only.

## Cross-Engine Interfaces
Movement Intelligence -> Exercise Metadata
Exercise Metadata -> Joint Stress
Exercise Metadata -> Stimulus-to-Fatigue
Exercise Metadata -> Recovery
Exercise Metadata -> Periodization
Exercise Metadata -> Coaching Explanation

3.name: PROGRESSION & REGRESSION ENGINE
3.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic engine governing advancement and simplification of exercises based on readiness, competency, recovery and performance.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: progression_regression
primary_key: progression_rule_id

fields:
  progression_rule_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  prerequisite_checks: list
  progression_target: string
  regression_target: string
  advancement_thresholds:
    technique_score: integer
    rir: integer
    completion_rate: float
  failure_conditions: list
```

## Decision Inputs
- exercise_id
- movement_id
- technique_score
- RIR
- RPE
- repetition_target
- completed_reps
- pain_flag
- readiness_score
- recovery_score

## Progression Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| P001 | Technique ≥9/10 for two sessions | Progress load |
| P002 | Top of rep range achieved twice | Increase resistance |
| P003 | Bodyweight exercise mastered | Advance variation |
| P004 | Recovery score <60 | Hold progression |
| P005 | Pain flag present | Block progression |

## Regression Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| R001 | Technique <7/10 | Easier variation |
| R002 | Pain during execution | Immediate regression |
| R003 | Unable to reach minimum reps | Reduce load |
| R004 | Mobility limitation | Select compatible regression |
| R005 | Medical restriction | Use rehabilitation movement |

## Progression Hierarchy
1. Improve technique
2. Complete prescribed volume
3. Reach rep ceiling
4. Increase load
5. Increase complexity
6. Increase instability only if goal requires

## Validation
- Every exercise_id must define at least one progression and one regression.
- Progression must never bypass prerequisite movement competency.
- Regression must preserve primary movement_id whenever possible.

## Interfaces
Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata
- Readiness
- Recovery Capacity

Provides:
- Periodization
- Plateau Engine
- Coaching Explanation

4.name: JOINT STRESS ENGINE
4.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic model for estimating relative mechanical stress imposed on major joints during exercise execution. This engine informs exercise selection, injury modification, recovery planning, and programming decisions.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: joint_stress
primary_key: joint_stress_profile

fields:
  joint_stress_profile: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  joints:
    shoulder: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    elbow: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    wrist: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    spine: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    hip: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    knee: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
    ankle: minimal|low|moderate|high|very_high
  axial_loading: boolean
  shear_loading: boolean
  compression_loading: boolean
```

## Stress Scale
| Score | Meaning |
|---|---|
| minimal | Negligible mechanical stress |
| low | Appropriate for high frequency |
| moderate | Standard training stress |
| high | Requires recovery consideration |
| very_high | Limited exposure and careful monitoring |

## Sample Profiles

### JST_SQUAT_001
```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
shoulder: low
elbow: minimal
wrist: low
spine: high
hip: high
knee: high
ankle: moderate
axial_loading: true
shear_loading: moderate
compression_loading: true
```

### JST_HPUSH_001
```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
shoulder: moderate
elbow: moderate
wrist: low
spine: low
hip: minimal
knee: minimal
ankle: minimal
axial_loading: false
shear_loading: low
compression_loading: low
```

## Deterministic Rules
1. Every exercise SHALL reference exactly one joint_stress_profile.
2. Joint stress values describe the exercise, not the athlete.
3. Injury Engine may filter exercises using these profiles.
4. Programming shall limit cumulative high and very_high stress exposures.

## Validation
- Every joint_stress_profile must be unique.
- Referenced exercise_id must exist.
- Stress values must use canonical scale only.

## Interfaces
Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata

Provides:
- Recovery
- Injury Engine
- Periodization
- Exercise Selection
- Coaching Explanation

JST_SQUAT_001.notes: Axial load through spine and knee flexion under load; technique-sensitive.

JST_HINGE_001.notes: High shear on lumbar spine if lordosis not maintained.

JST_HPUSH_001.notes: Anterior shoulder stress increases with excessive range/flare.

JST_VPUSH_001.notes: Overhead position increases impingement risk in limited thoracic mobility.

JST_HPULL_001.notes: Generally joint-friendly; low shear when scapular retraction maintained.

JST_VPULL_001.notes: Low stress when full ROM controlled; higher with kipping variations.

JST_LUNGE_001.notes: Unilateral load increases knee tracking demand; ankle mobility dependent.

JST_CARRY_001.notes: Low dynamic stress; isometric spinal loading, generally very safe.

JST_ROT_001.notes: Rotational shear on spine; controlled tempo reduces risk.

JST_ISO_ARM_001.notes: Single-joint, low systemic and spinal stress.

JST_ISO_LEG_001.notes: Machine-supported single-joint stress; low spinal involvement.

JST_ISO_SHLD_001.notes: Light loads typical; rotator cuff sensitive if fatigued/overused.

JST_ISO_CALF_001.notes: Minimal systemic stress; achilles-sensitive at high volume.

JST_CORE_001.notes: Flexion-based variations increase lumbar disc load at high volume.

JST_COND_001.notes: Impact/repetition-driven stress; varies widely by specific exercise.

5.name: STIMULUS-TO-FATIGUE (STF) ENGINE
5.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic model estimating the ratio between hypertrophic/strength stimulus and systemic/local fatigue generated by an exercise. Used to optimize exercise selection, weekly volume, recovery and periodization.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: stimulus_to_fatigue
primary_key: fatigue_profile

fields:
  fatigue_profile: string
  exercise_id: string
 movement_id: string
  stimulus_score: 1-10
  local_fatigue: 1-10
  systemic_fatigue: 1-10
  technical_fatigue: 1-10
  recovery_cost: 1-10
  stf_rating: very_low|low|moderate|high|excellent
```

## STF Rating
| Rating | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| excellent | High stimulus with low recovery cost |
| high | Strong stimulus with manageable fatigue |
| moderate | Balanced stimulus and fatigue |
| low | Fatigue outweighs stimulus for most goals |
| very_low | Generally poor exercise economy |

## Sample Profiles

### STF_SQUAT_001
```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
stimulus_score: 9
local_fatigue: 8
systemic_fatigue: 9
technical_fatigue: 7
recovery_cost: 9
stf_rating: high
```

### STF_HPUSH_001
```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
stimulus_score: 8
local_fatigue: 6
systemic_fatigue: 4
technical_fatigue: 4
recovery_cost: 5
stf_rating: excellent
```

## Deterministic Rules
1. Every exercise SHALL reference one fatigue_profile.
2. STF values describe intrinsic exercise characteristics, not athlete adaptation.
3. Programming SHOULD prioritize excellent/high STF exercises when all other constraints are equal.
4. Recovery and Recovery Capacity engines SHALL incorporate recovery_cost.
5. Plateau and Weak Point engines MAY intentionally select lower STF exercises for variation.

## Validation
- fatigue_profile identifiers must be unique.
- Referenced exercise_id must exist in Exercise Metadata.
- stimulus_score and fatigue metrics use a 1–10 integer scale.
- stf_rating must use the canonical enumeration.

## Interfaces
Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata
- Joint Stress

Provides:
- Recovery
- Recovery Capacity
- Periodization
- Plateau Engine
- Exercise Selection
- Coaching Explanation

6.name: RECOVERY ENGINE
6.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic engine responsible for estimating recovery requirements following training exposure. The Recovery Engine integrates exercise characteristics, accumulated fatigue, joint stress, readiness, sleep, nutrition and training history to determine when an athlete is prepared for subsequent training.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: recovery
primary_key: recovery_profile_id

fields:
  recovery_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  minimum_recovery_hours: integer
  recommended_recovery_hours: integer
  fatigue_source:
    local: integer
    systemic: integer
    neural: integer
  recovery_modifiers:
    sleep_quality: poor|fair|good|excellent
    nutrition: poor|adequate|optimal
    soreness: none|mild|moderate|high
    stress_level: low|moderate|high
  readiness_threshold: integer
  recovery_status: recovered|partial|insufficient
```

## Canonical Recovery Scale

| Score | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| 0-39 | Insufficient Recovery |
| 40-69 | Partial Recovery |
| 70-89 | Recovered |
| 90-100 | Fully Recovered |

## Recovery Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| REC001 | Recovery score <40 | Do not train affected movement |
| REC002 | Recovery score 40-69 | Reduce load or volume |
| REC003 | Recovery score >=70 | Normal programming |
| REC004 | Joint stress = very_high | Extend recovery window |
| REC005 | Sleep <6 h for 2 nights | Reduce weekly workload |

## Sample Profiles

### REC_SQUAT_001
```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
minimum_recovery_hours: 48
recommended_recovery_hours: 72
fatigue_source:
  local: 8
  systemic: 9
  neural: 7
readiness_threshold: 75
recovery_status: recovered
```

### REC_HPUSH_001
```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
minimum_recovery_hours: 24
recommended_recovery_hours: 48
fatigue_source:
  local: 6
  systemic: 4
  neural: 3
readiness_threshold: 65
recovery_status: recovered
```

## Validation

- Every exercise SHALL reference one recovery profile.
- Recovery windows shall never be negative.
- Readiness threshold must be 0-100.
- Recovery consumes Joint Stress and STF outputs; it shall not redefine them.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata
- Joint Stress
- Stimulus-to-Fatigue
- Readiness
- Recovery Capacity

Provides:
- Periodization
- Plateau Engine
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation

7.name: SKILL ENGINE
7.spec_text:
## Purpose
The Skill Engine classifies the technical competency required to safely and effectively perform an exercise. It separates technical complexity from strength and fatigue, allowing programming decisions based on movement proficiency.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: skill
primary_key: skill_profile_id

fields:
  skill_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  skill_level: novice|beginner|intermediate|advanced|expert
  coordination: 1-10
  balance: 1-10
  timing: 1-10
  motor_control: 1-10
  learning_sessions_estimate: integer
  coaching_priority:
    - setup
    - bracing
    - execution
    - tempo
    - breathing
```

## Canonical Skill Scale

| Level | Description |
|---|---|
| novice | No prior competency required |
| beginner | Simple technique with minimal coaching |
| intermediate | Moderate coordination and consistency required |
| advanced | High technical precision required |
| expert | Elite technical proficiency required |

## Sample Profiles

### SKL_SQUAT_001
```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
skill_level: intermediate
coordination: 7
balance: 6
timing: 5
motor_control: 7
learning_sessions_estimate: 8
coaching_priority:
- setup
- bracing
- depth
- bar_path
```

### SKL_HPUSH_001
```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
skill_level: beginner
coordination: 4
balance: 2
timing: 3
motor_control: 4
learning_sessions_estimate: 3
coaching_priority:
- setup
- grip
- range_of_motion
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every exercise SHALL reference one skill_profile_id.
2. Skill describes technical demand, not training age.
3. Progression Engine SHALL verify minimum skill competency before advancing.
4. Readiness MAY temporarily reduce permitted skill level under fatigue.
5. Coaching Explanation SHALL expose coaching priorities.

## Validation

- skill_profile_id must be unique.
- Referenced exercise_id must exist.
- Skill metrics use integer values from 1 to 10.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata
- Progression & Regression
- Recovery

Provides:
- Readiness
- Periodization
- Coaching Explanation
- Exercise Selection

8.name: BIOMECHANICS ENGINE
8.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic biomechanical model describing how forces, levers, joint moments and body geometry influence exercise execution. This engine defines intrinsic mechanical characteristics independent of individual athlete performance.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: biomechanics
primary_key: biomechanics_profile_id

fields:
  biomechanics_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  primary_force_vector: vertical|horizontal|diagonal|rotational
  resistance_curve: ascending|descending|bell|constant
  lever_class: first|second|third|mixed
  stability_requirement: low|moderate|high
  balance_requirement: low|moderate|high
  center_of_mass_shift: minimal|moderate|significant
  external_moment_arm: short|moderate|long
  internal_moment_arm_dependency: low|moderate|high
```

## Canonical Definitions

| Property | Values |
|---|---|
| Force Vector | vertical, horizontal, diagonal, rotational |
| Resistance Curve | ascending, descending, bell, constant |
| Stability | low, moderate, high |
| Moment Arm | short, moderate, long |

## Sample Profiles

### BIO_SQUAT_001

```yaml
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
primary_force_vector: vertical
resistance_curve: ascending
lever_class: third
stability_requirement: high
balance_requirement: moderate
center_of_mass_shift: moderate
external_moment_arm: long
internal_moment_arm_dependency: high
```

### BIO_HPUSH_001

```yaml
exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
movement_id: horizontal_push
primary_force_vector: vertical
resistance_curve: constant
lever_class: third
stability_requirement: moderate
balance_requirement: low
center_of_mass_shift: minimal
external_moment_arm: moderate
internal_moment_arm_dependency: moderate
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every exercise SHALL reference one biomechanics profile.
2. Biomechanical properties are intrinsic and athlete-independent.
3. Exercise Selection MAY compare exercises using biomechanical similarity.
4. Joint Stress SHALL consume moment-arm information when estimating stress.
5. Coaching Explanation SHALL expose biomechanical rationale where relevant.

## Validation

- biomechanics_profile_id must be unique.
- exercise_id must exist in Exercise Metadata.
- Enumerated fields shall use canonical values only.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Movement Intelligence
- Exercise Metadata

Provides:
- Joint Stress
- Exercise Selection
- Periodization
- Coaching Explanation

9.name: READINESS ENGINE
9.spec_text:
## Purpose
Deterministic engine that determines whether an athlete is prepared to perform a planned training session based on recovery, fatigue, pain, sleep, stress, and movement competency. Readiness modifies programming but does not redefine exercise characteristics.

### Schema
```yaml
engine: readiness
primary_key: readiness_profile_id

fields:
  readiness_profile_id: string
  readiness_score: 0-100
  sleep_score: 0-100
  recovery_score: 0-100
  soreness_score: 0-100
  stress_score: 0-100
  pain_flag: boolean
  illness_flag: boolean
  recommendation: train|modified_train|deload|rest
```

## Readiness Zones

| Score | Status | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 90-100 | Optimal | Full training |
| 75-89 | Ready | Planned session |
| 60-74 | Caution | Reduce volume/intensity |
| 40-59 | Limited | Technique or recovery session |
| 0-39 | Not Ready | Rest or rehabilitation |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Output |
|---|---|---|
| RD001 | pain_flag = true | rest |
| RD002 | illness_flag = true | rest |
| RD003 | recovery_score < 40 | deload |
| RD004 | sleep_score < 50 for 2 days | modified_train |
| RD005 | readiness_score >= 75 | train |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
readiness_profile_id: READY_DEFAULT
readiness_score: 82
sleep_score: 85
recovery_score: 78
soreness_score: 70
stress_score: 80
pain_flag: false
illness_flag: false
recommendation: train
```

## Validation

- Scores SHALL be integers from 0 to 100.
- pain_flag or illness_flag overrides readiness score.
- Recommendation must be one of the canonical outputs.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Recovery
- Joint Stress
- Stimulus-to-Fatigue
- Skill
- Biomechanics

Provides:
- Periodization
- Programming Engine
- Plateau Engine
- Coaching Explanation

10.name: RECOVERY CAPACITY ENGINE
10.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Recovery Capacity Engine estimates the maximum recoverable workload an athlete can tolerate over time while maintaining positive adaptation.

Unlike the Recovery Engine, which determines whether an athlete has recovered from previous training, the Recovery Capacity Engine determines how much training the athlete can successfully recover from.

This engine governs:

- Weekly volume allocation
- Frequency recommendations
- Deload scheduling
- Maximum Recoverable Volume (MRV)
- Maximum Adaptive Volume (MAV)
- Minimum Effective Volume (MEV)
- Fatigue accumulation
- Programming constraints

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: recovery_capacity
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: recovery_capacity_profile_id

fields:
  recovery_capacity_profile_id: string
  athlete_profile:
    training_age: beginner|intermediate|advanced
    age_group: youth|adult|masters
  recovery_inputs:
    sleep_score: integer
    nutrition_score: integer
    stress_score: integer
    readiness_score: integer
    recovery_score: integer
    fatigue_score: integer
  capacity:
    MEV: integer
    MAV_low: integer
    MAV_high: integer
    MRV: integer
  modifiers:
    illness: boolean
    injury: boolean
    caloric_deficit: boolean
    caloric_surplus: boolean
  recommendation:
    volume_adjustment: decrease|maintain|increase
    frequency_adjustment: decrease|maintain|increase
    deload_required: boolean
```

## Canonical Volume Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| MEV | Minimum Effective Volume |
| MAV | Maximum Adaptive Volume |
| MRV | Maximum Recoverable Volume |

## Recovery Capacity Scale

| Capacity Score | Interpretation |
|---------------|----------------|
| 90–100 | Exceptional Recovery Capacity |
| 75–89 | High Recovery Capacity |
| 60–74 | Normal Recovery Capacity |
| 40–59 | Reduced Capacity |
| 20–39 | Poor Capacity |
| 0–19 | Recovery Compromised |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
recovery_capacity_profile_id: RC_DEFAULT

athlete_profile:
  training_age: intermediate
  age_group: adult

recovery_inputs:
  sleep_score: 84
  nutrition_score: 88
  stress_score: 70
  readiness_score: 81
  recovery_score: 78
  fatigue_score: 35

capacity:
  MEV: 10
  MAV_low: 14
  MAV_high: 20
  MRV: 24

modifiers:
  illness: false
  injury: false
  caloric_deficit: false
  caloric_surplus: true

recommendation:
  volume_adjustment: maintain
  frequency_adjustment: maintain
  deload_required: false
```

## Volume Adjustment Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|------|-----------|--------|
| RC001 | Capacity ≥90 | Increase volume |
| RC002 | Capacity 75–89 | Maintain or slightly increase |
| RC003 | Capacity 60–74 | Maintain |
| RC004 | Capacity 40–59 | Reduce weekly volume by 10–20% |
| RC005 | Capacity <40 | Reduce volume by 30–50% |

## Frequency Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|------|-----------|--------|
| RF001 | Recovery Capacity High | Increase frequency if appropriate |
| RF002 | Recovery Capacity Moderate | Maintain frequency |
| RF003 | Recovery Capacity Low | Reduce frequency |

## Deload Rules

Recommend a deload if any apply:

- Recovery Capacity <40
- Readiness <50 for three consecutive sessions
- Recovery Score <50 for one week
- Fatigue exceeds MRV
- Joint Stress exceeds threshold

## Deterministic Rules

1. Recovery Capacity SHALL NOT modify intrinsic exercise metadata.
2. Recovery Capacity SHALL consume outputs from Recovery Engine.
3. MRV MUST always be greater than MAV.
4. MAV MUST always be greater than MEV.
5. Deload recommendations SHALL override progression.
6. Illness SHALL reduce Recovery Capacity.
7. Severe caloric deficit SHALL reduce MRV.
8. Chronic poor sleep SHALL reduce recovery score.
9. Injury SHALL reduce effective MRV.
10. Periodization SHALL consult Recovery Capacity before increasing workload.

## Validation

- recovery_capacity_profile_id MUST be unique.
- MEV < MAV < MRV.
- Scores MUST remain between 0 and 100.
- Recommendation values MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Recovery Engine
- Readiness Engine
- Joint Stress Engine
- Stimulus-to-Fatigue Engine
- Skill Engine
- Biomechanics Engine

Provides:
- Periodization Engine
- Plateau Engine
- Weak Point Engine
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Volume Recommendation Engine

11.name: PLATEAU ENGINE
11.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Plateau Engine detects stagnation in performance or adaptation and recommends deterministic interventions before unnecessary program changes are made.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: plateau
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: plateau_profile_id

fields:
  plateau_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  plateau_status: none|suspected|confirmed
  consecutive_sessions: integer
  performance_delta_percent: float
  bodyweight_delta_percent: float
  recovery_capacity_score: integer
  readiness_score: integer
  intervention:
    load: maintain|increase|decrease
    volume: maintain|increase|decrease
    frequency: maintain|increase|decrease
    deload: boolean
    exercise_variation: boolean
```

## Detection Rules

| Rule | Condition | Result |
|------|-----------|--------|
| PL001 | No measurable progress for 3 sessions | Suspected plateau |
| PL002 | No progress for 6 sessions | Confirmed plateau |
| PL003 | Recovery Capacity <40 | Recovery limitation, not plateau |
| PL004 | Readiness <60 | Delay plateau diagnosis |
| PL005 | Pain or injury present | Refer to Injury Engine |

## Intervention Priority

1. Verify adherence
2. Verify recovery
3. Verify nutrition
4. Deload if required
5. Adjust volume
6. Adjust frequency
7. Change exercise variation
8. Modify periodization

## Sample Profile

```yaml
plateau_profile_id: PL_DEFAULT
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
plateau_status: suspected
consecutive_sessions: 3
performance_delta_percent: 0.0
bodyweight_delta_percent: 0.2
recovery_capacity_score: 78
readiness_score: 81
intervention:
  load: maintain
  volume: increase
  frequency: maintain
  deload: false
  exercise_variation: false
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Plateau SHALL NOT be declared until adherence is verified.
2. Recovery deficiency SHALL take precedence over plateau diagnosis.
3. Deload recommendations override progression.
4. Exercise variation SHALL preserve movement_id whenever possible.
5. Plateau history SHALL be retained for longitudinal analysis.

## Validation

- plateau_profile_id MUST be unique.
- performance_delta_percent may be negative or positive.
- Plateau status MUST use canonical values.
- Intervention values MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Progression & Regression
- Recovery Capacity
- Recovery
- Readiness
- Periodization

Provides:
- Weak Point Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine

12.name: WEAK POINT ENGINE
12.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Weak Point Engine identifies limiting muscles, movement patterns, technical deficiencies, or mobility restrictions that reduce performance. It recommends deterministic corrective actions while preserving the overall training objective.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: weak_point
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: weak_point_profile_id

fields:
  weak_point_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  weak_point_type:
    muscle|movement|mobility|stability|skill
  severity:
    low|moderate|high|critical
  affected_region: string
  evidence:
    failed_range: boolean
    technical_breakdown: boolean
    asymmetry: boolean
    plateau: boolean
  corrective_actions:
    accessory_work: list
    mobility: list
    cueing: list
    progression_change: string
  reassessment_sessions: integer
```

## Weak Point Categories

| Category | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Muscle | Insufficient force production |
| Movement | Inefficient movement pattern |
| Mobility | ROM limitation |
| Stability | Inability to stabilize joints |
| Skill | Technical execution deficiency |

## Detection Rules

| Rule | Condition | Result |
|------|-----------|--------|
| WP001 | Repeated failure at same ROM | Mobility Weak Point |
| WP002 | Technique breakdown with adequate strength | Skill Weak Point |
| WP003 | Consistent asymmetry | Stability Weak Point |
| WP004 | Plateau isolated to one muscle group | Muscle Weak Point |
| WP005 | Pain present | Refer to Injury Engine |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
weak_point_profile_id: WP_SQUAT_001
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
weak_point_type: mobility
severity: moderate
affected_region: ankle
evidence:
  failed_range: true
  technical_breakdown: true
  asymmetry: false
  plateau: false
corrective_actions:
  accessory_work:
    - tibialis_raise
    - split_squat
  mobility:
    - ankle_dorsiflexion_drill
  cueing:
    - knees_over_toes
  progression_change: maintain_load
reassessment_sessions: 4
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Weak points SHALL be supported by objective evidence.
2. Injury SHALL take precedence over weak point correction.
3. Corrective work SHALL preserve the primary movement objective.
4. Reassess after the configured number of sessions.
5. Corrective exercises SHALL reference Exercise Metadata IDs.

## Validation

- weak_point_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Severity MUST use canonical values.
- Corrective actions MUST reference valid exercise identifiers where applicable.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Plateau Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Progression & Regression
- Exercise Metadata
- Movement Intelligence
- Readiness

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Pairing Engine

13.name: PAIRING ENGINE
13.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Pairing Engine deterministically selects compatible exercise pairings for supersets, circuits, antagonistic work, pre-exhaust, post-exhaust and efficiency blocks while minimizing interference and respecting recovery constraints.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: pairing
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: pairing_profile_id

fields:
  pairing_profile_id: string
  primary_exercise_id: string
  secondary_exercise_id: string
  pairing_type: antagonist|agonist|upper_lower|push_pull|compound_isolation|mobility|conditioning
  compatibility_score: 0-100
  fatigue_interference: low|moderate|high
  joint_overlap: low|moderate|high
  equipment_conflict: boolean
  recommended_rest_seconds: integer
```

## Pairing Types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| antagonist | Opposing muscle groups |
| push_pull | Push followed by pull |
| upper_lower | Alternating body regions |
| compound_isolation | Compound followed by isolation |
| mobility | Exercise followed by corrective drill |
| conditioning | Exercise paired with conditioning |

## Compatibility Rules

| Rule | Condition | Result |
|---|---|---|
| PR001 | Opposing movement patterns | Preferred |
| PR002 | Same high joint stress | Avoid |
| PR003 | High STF + Low STF | Preferred |
| PR004 | Equipment conflict | Reduce compatibility |
| PR005 | Readiness <60 | Avoid metabolically demanding pairings |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
pairing_profile_id: PAIR_PUSH_PULL_001
primary_exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
secondary_exercise_id: seated_cable_row
pairing_type: push_pull
compatibility_score: 92
fatigue_interference: low
joint_overlap: low
equipment_conflict: false
recommended_rest_seconds: 75
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Pairings SHALL preserve primary programming goals.
2. Injury and Joint Stress restrictions override pairing recommendations.
3. Pairings SHALL reference valid exercise_id values.
4. Compatibility SHALL decrease with excessive fatigue overlap.
5. Recovery and Readiness SHALL be evaluated before prescribing supersets.

## Validation

- pairing_profile_id MUST be unique.
- compatibility_score MUST be 0-100.
- Pairing types SHALL use canonical values.
- Referenced exercises MUST exist.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Exercise Metadata
- Movement Intelligence
- Joint Stress
- Stimulus-to-Fatigue
- Recovery
- Recovery Capacity
- Weak Point

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Conflict Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine

14.name: CONFLICT ENGINE
14.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Conflict Engine detects deterministic conflicts before a program is finalized, including exercise redundancy, excessive fatigue overlap, recovery violations, equipment conflicts, scheduling issues, and contraindications.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: conflict
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: conflict_profile_id

fields:
  conflict_profile_id: string
  conflict_type: movement|fatigue|joint_stress|equipment|schedule|medical
  severity: low|moderate|high|critical
  primary_reference: string
  secondary_reference: string
  detected: boolean
  resolution:
    action: remove|replace|reschedule|reduce_volume|reduce_intensity
    replacement_id: string|null
```

## Conflict Types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| movement | Duplicate movement emphasis |
| fatigue | Excessive cumulative fatigue |
| joint_stress | Repeated high joint loading |
| equipment | Equipment unavailable or conflicting |
| schedule | Recovery window violation |
| medical | Contraindication or injury conflict |

## Detection Rules

| Rule | Condition | Result |
|---|---|---|
| CF001 | Same movement pattern repeated beyond plan | Movement conflict |
| CF002 | STF + Recovery exceed MRV | Fatigue conflict |
| CF003 | Two high joint stress exercises consecutively | Joint conflict |
| CF004 | Required equipment unavailable | Equipment conflict |
| CF005 | Contraindication present | Medical conflict |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
conflict_profile_id: CONFLICT_001
conflict_type: fatigue
severity: high
primary_reference: barbell_back_squat
secondary_reference: conventional_deadlift
detected: true
resolution:
  action: reschedule
  replacement_id: null
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Critical conflicts SHALL block program generation.
2. Medical conflicts override all other decisions.
3. Resolution SHALL preserve training goals where possible.
4. Conflicts SHALL be evaluated before Pairing and Periodization output.

## Validation

- conflict_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Severity SHALL use canonical values.
- Resolution actions SHALL use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Pairing Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Joint Stress Engine
- Stimulus-to-Fatigue Engine
- Readiness Engine
- Exercise Metadata

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine

15.name: ADHERENCE ENGINE
15.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Adherence Engine evaluates how consistently a user follows the prescribed program and recommends adjustments that maximize long-term compliance without compromising training objectives.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: adherence
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: adherence_profile_id

fields:
  adherence_profile_id: string
  adherence_score: 0-100
  attendance_rate: float
  workout_completion_rate: float
  exercise_substitution_rate: float
  schedule_consistency: low|moderate|high
  missed_sessions: integer
  preferred_training_days: list
  recommendation:
    volume_adjustment: decrease|maintain|increase
    session_duration: shorten|maintain|extend
    exercise_complexity: decrease|maintain|increase
```

## Adherence Scale

| Score | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| 90-100 | Excellent |
| 75-89 | Good |
| 60-74 | Fair |
| 40-59 | Poor |
| 0-39 | Critical |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| AD001 | Adherence <60 | Reduce program complexity |
| AD002 | Missed ≥2 sessions/week | Reduce frequency |
| AD003 | Completion >90% for 4 weeks | Permit progression |
| AD004 | Frequent substitutions | Recommend preferred alternatives |
| AD005 | Low adherence + plateau | Prioritize adherence before progression |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
adherence_profile_id: AD_DEFAULT
adherence_score: 84
attendance_rate: 0.90
workout_completion_rate: 0.93
exercise_substitution_rate: 0.08
schedule_consistency: high
missed_sessions: 1
preferred_training_days:
  - monday
  - wednesday
  - friday
recommendation:
  volume_adjustment: maintain
  session_duration: maintain
  exercise_complexity: maintain
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Adherence SHALL be evaluated before diagnosing a plateau.
2. Low adherence SHALL block automatic progression.
3. Program simplification SHALL precede volume increases for poor adherence.
4. Recommendations SHALL preserve primary training goals whenever possible.

## Validation

- adherence_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Scores MUST remain between 0 and 100.
- Rates MUST be between 0.0 and 1.0.
- Recommendation values MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Plateau Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Progression & Regression
- Readiness Engine
- Exercise Metadata

Provides:
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Programming Engine

16.name: TEMPO & INTENT ENGINE
16.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Tempo & Intent Engine defines the prescribed execution speed and muscular intent for every exercise. It standardizes eccentric, pause, concentric, and lockout timing while specifying the desired force production strategy for different training goals.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: tempo_intent
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: tempo_profile_id

fields:
  tempo_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  movement_id: string
  tempo: string
  eccentric_seconds: integer
  stretch_pause_seconds: integer
  concentric_seconds: integer
  lockout_pause_seconds: integer
  intent:
    explosive|controlled|max_force|hypertrophy|rehabilitation
  cue_priority: list
```

## Canonical Intent Types

| Intent | Description |
|---|---|
| explosive | Maximal acceleration |
| controlled | Smooth, consistent execution |
| max_force | Maximum force production |
| hypertrophy | Controlled tension throughout ROM |
| rehabilitation | Slow, deliberate movement |

## Tempo Rules

| Goal | Recommended Tempo |
|---|---|
| Strength | 2-0-X-1 |
| Hypertrophy | 3-1-2-0 |
| Power | X-0-X-0 |
| Rehabilitation | 4-2-2-2 |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
tempo_profile_id: TMP_SQUAT_001
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
movement_id: squat
tempo: "3-1-2-0"
eccentric_seconds: 3
stretch_pause_seconds: 1
concentric_seconds: 2
lockout_pause_seconds: 0
intent: hypertrophy
cue_priority:
  - brace
  - control_descent
  - drive_up
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every exercise SHALL reference one tempo profile.
2. Intent SHALL align with programming goals.
3. Explosive intent SHALL only be prescribed when readiness permits.
4. Tempo changes SHALL not alter intrinsic exercise metadata.
5. Coaching cues SHALL correspond to the selected intent.

## Validation

- tempo_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Tempo SHALL use canonical notation.
- Time values MUST be non-negative integers.
- Intent SHALL use canonical values.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Exercise Metadata
- Movement Intelligence
- Readiness Engine
- Skill Engine
- Periodization Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine

17.name: PERIODIZATION ENGINE
17.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Periodization Engine organizes training into macrocycles, mesocycles, and microcycles using deterministic rules. It sequences workload, recovery, progression, and deloads while integrating outputs from all upstream engines.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: periodization
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: periodization_profile_id

fields:
  periodization_profile_id: string
  goal: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|fat_loss|general_fitness
  macrocycle_weeks: integer
  mesocycle_weeks: integer
  microcycle_days: integer
  progression_model: linear|double_progression|undulating|block|conjugate
  deload_frequency_weeks: integer
  volume_strategy: increase|maintain|decrease|wave
  intensity_strategy: increase|maintain|decrease|wave
```

## Canonical Cycle Definitions

| Cycle | Definition |
|---|---|
| Macrocycle | Long-term training plan |
| Mesocycle | Focused training block |
| Microcycle | Weekly programming unit |

## Planning Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| PD001 | Recovery Capacity low | Reduce planned volume |
| PD002 | Plateau confirmed | Change mesocycle strategy |
| PD003 | Readiness consistently high | Permit progression |
| PD004 | Deload interval reached | Schedule deload |
| PD005 | Adherence poor | Simplify progression model |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
periodization_profile_id: PER_HYP_001
goal: hypertrophy
macrocycle_weeks: 24
mesocycle_weeks: 6
microcycle_days: 7
progression_model: double_progression
deload_frequency_weeks: 6
volume_strategy: increase
intensity_strategy: wave
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every program SHALL belong to one macrocycle.
2. Every macrocycle SHALL contain one or more mesocycles.
3. Every mesocycle SHALL contain one or more microcycles.
4. Deloads SHALL override progression when triggered.
5. Periodization SHALL consume Recovery Capacity before increasing workload.
6. Progression SHALL respect Skill, Readiness and Adherence outputs.

## Validation

- periodization_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Macrocycle > Mesocycle > Microcycle duration.
- Deload frequency MUST be greater than zero.
- Strategy values SHALL use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Plateau Engine
- Adherence Engine
- Readiness Engine
- Progression & Regression
- Tempo & Intent Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Plateau Engine

18.name: COACHING EXPLANATION ENGINE
18.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Coaching Explanation Engine converts deterministic decisions made by the knowledge base into transparent, human-readable explanations. Every recommendation must be traceable to upstream engine outputs.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: coaching_explanation
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: explanation_id

fields:
  explanation_id: string
  recommendation: string
  rationale: list
  evidence:
    engine: string
    reference_id: string
  confidence_score: 0-100
  safety_notice: string|null
  user_message: string
```

## Explanation Principles

| Principle | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Traceability | Every recommendation references source engines |
| Determinism | Same inputs produce same explanation |
| Safety | Surface warnings before recommendations |
| Clarity | Explain technical terms in plain language |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| CE001 | Safety conflict exists | Explain warning first |
| CE002 | Plateau detected | Explain why intervention changed |
| CE003 | Deload prescribed | Explain fatigue and recovery evidence |
| CE004 | Exercise substituted | Explain replacement rationale |
| CE005 | Progression blocked | Explain limiting factors |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
explanation_id: EXP_001
recommendation: Reduce lower-body volume by 15%
rationale:
  - Recovery Capacity below target
  - Joint stress accumulation elevated
evidence:
  engine: recovery_capacity
  reference_id: RC_DEFAULT
confidence_score: 94
safety_notice: null
user_message: Reduced volume is recommended because your current recovery capacity is below the programmed target while lower-body joint stress remains elevated.
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Explanations SHALL never invent evidence.
2. Every recommendation SHALL cite at least one upstream engine.
3. Safety notices SHALL take precedence over performance guidance.
4. Confidence scores SHALL reflect supporting evidence only.

## Validation

- explanation_id MUST be unique.
- confidence_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- recommendation and user_message MUST be populated.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- All reasoning engines

Provides:
- AI Coaching Layer
- User Interface
- Reports
- Audit Logs

19.name: PROGRAMMING ENGINE
19.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Programming Engine synthesizes outputs from all upstream reasoning engines into a deterministic training prescription. It does not generate recommendations independently; instead, it assembles validated decisions into an executable program.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: programming
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: program_id

fields:
  program_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  goal: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|fat_loss|general_fitness
  exercises: list
  sets: list
  reps: list
  load_strategy: fixed|percentage|rir|rpe
  frequency_per_week: integer
  weekly_volume_sets: integer
  progression_model: string
  deload_required: boolean
  safety_flags: list
```

## Assembly Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| PG001 | Critical conflict present | Block program generation |
| PG002 | Readiness below threshold | Reduce intensity/volume |
| PG003 | Recovery Capacity low | Cap weekly volume |
| PG004 | Plateau confirmed | Apply approved intervention |
| PG005 | Weak point detected | Insert corrective work |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
program_id: PROG_HYP_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
goal: hypertrophy
exercises:
  - barbell_back_squat
  - dumbbell_bench_press
  - seated_cable_row
sets: [4,4,3]
reps: ["6-8","8-10","10-12"]
load_strategy: rir
frequency_per_week: 4
weekly_volume_sets: 16
progression_model: double_progression
deload_required: false
safety_flags: []
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Programming SHALL consume upstream engine outputs only.
2. Safety constraints SHALL override performance objectives.
3. Every exercise SHALL reference Exercise Metadata.
4. Volume SHALL remain within MEV-MRV boundaries.
5. Programs SHALL be reproducible for identical inputs.

## Validation

- program_id MUST be unique.
- Weekly volume MUST satisfy Recovery Capacity limits.
- Every referenced exercise MUST exist.
- Safety flags MUST be resolved or acknowledged.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Engines 1–18

Provides:
- AI Coaching Layer
- Workout Execution
- Analytics

20.name: EXERCISE SELECTION ENGINE
20.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Exercise Selection Engine deterministically selects exercises that satisfy the programming objective while respecting movement requirements, skill level, recovery status, equipment availability, joint stress limits, contraindications, and weak-point priorities.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: exercise_selection
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: selection_profile_id

fields:
  selection_profile_id: string
  goal: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|fat_loss|general_fitness
  movement_id: string
  selected_exercise_id: string
  candidate_exercises: list
  selection_score: integer
  exclusion_reasons: list
  equipment_required: list
  prerequisites:
    minimum_skill: novice|beginner|intermediate|advanced|expert
    readiness_score: integer
```

## Selection Priority

| Priority | Rule |
|---:|---|
|1|Safety and contraindications|
|2|Movement pattern match|
|3|Training goal alignment|
|4|Recovery and readiness|
|5|Skill compatibility|
|6|Equipment availability|
|7|Weak-point integration|

## Deterministic Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| ES001 | Medical or safety conflict | Exclude exercise |
| ES002 | Skill below requirement | Select regression |
| ES003 | Joint stress exceeds threshold | Choose lower-stress alternative |
| ES004 | Equipment unavailable | Select equivalent variation |
| ES005 | Weak point identified | Prefer compatible corrective option |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
selection_profile_id: SEL_001
goal: hypertrophy
movement_id: horizontal_push
selected_exercise_id: dumbbell_bench_press
candidate_exercises:
  - dumbbell_bench_press
  - machine_chest_press
selection_score: 95
exclusion_reasons:
  - barbell_bench_press: shoulder_joint_stress
equipment_required:
  - adjustable_bench
  - dumbbells
prerequisites:
  minimum_skill: beginner
  readiness_score: 65
```

## Validation

- selection_profile_id MUST be unique.
- selected_exercise_id MUST exist in Exercise Metadata.
- selection_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- All exclusions MUST include a deterministic reason.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Exercise Metadata
- Movement Intelligence
- Joint Stress
- Recovery Capacity
- Readiness
- Weak Point
- Conflict
- Programming

Provides:
- Workout Execution
- AI Coaching Layer
- Analytics

21.name: LOAD PRESCRIPTION ENGINE
21.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Load Prescription Engine deterministically selects the appropriate training load for every exercise using objective intensity models. It integrates athlete readiness, recovery, progression status, training goal, and historical performance to prescribe a safe and effective working load.

---

# Schema

```yaml
engine: load_prescription
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: load_profile_id

fields:
  load_profile_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  intensity_method: percentage_1rm|rpe|rir|velocity|fixed_load
  target_intensity: float
  target_reps: integer
  target_sets: integer
  readiness_modifier_percent: float
  recovery_modifier_percent: float
  final_load: float
  load_unit: kg|lb
```

## Intensity Hierarchy

| Priority | Source |
|---:|---|
|1|Medical and safety restrictions|
|2|Recovery Capacity|
|3|Readiness|
|4|Progression model|
|5|Training goal|
|6|Historical performance|

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| LP001 | Readiness <60 | Reduce prescribed load 5–15% |
| LP002 | Recovery Capacity low | Cap weekly intensity |
| LP003 | Plateau confirmed | Maintain load and modify variable indicated by Plateau Engine |
| LP004 | Deload active | Reduce intensity per periodization plan |
| LP005 | RPE exceeds target by >1 | Decrease next prescription |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
load_profile_id: LOAD_001
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
intensity_method: rir
target_intensity: 77.5
target_reps: 8
target_sets: 4
readiness_modifier_percent: -5
recovery_modifier_percent: 0
final_load: 110
load_unit: kg
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Load SHALL never exceed safety constraints.
2. Final load SHALL be reproducible for identical inputs.
3. Exercise metadata SHALL remain immutable.
4. Modifiers SHALL be applied in canonical order.
5. Load prescription SHALL support all approved intensity methods.

## Validation

- load_profile_id MUST be unique.
- target_intensity MUST be between 0 and 100 for percentage methods.
- target_sets and target_reps MUST be positive integers.
- final_load MUST be non-negative.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Programming Engine
- Exercise Selection Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Readiness Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Progression & Regression

Provides:
- Workout Execution
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Analytics

22.name: VOLUME ALLOCATION ENGINE
22.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Volume Allocation Engine deterministically distributes weekly training volume across movement patterns and muscle groups while ensuring workloads remain within MEV, MAV and MRV limits.

## Schema

```yaml
engine: volume_allocation
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: volume_profile_id

fields:
  volume_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  goal: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|fat_loss|general_fitness
  muscle_group: string
  weekly_sets: integer
  mev: integer
  mav_low: integer
  mav_high: integer
  mrv: integer
  allocation_status: below_mev|optimal|above_mav|above_mrv
```

## Allocation Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| VA001 | Weekly sets < MEV | Increase volume |
| VA002 | Weekly sets within MAV | Maintain |
| VA003 | Weekly sets > MAV | Monitor recovery |
| VA004 | Weekly sets > MRV | Reduce volume |
| VA005 | Recovery Capacity low | Reduce allocation before intensity |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
volume_profile_id: VOL_CHEST_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
goal: hypertrophy
muscle_group: chest
weekly_sets: 16
mev: 10
mav_low: 14
mav_high: 20
mrv: 24
allocation_status: optimal
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Volume SHALL remain within MEV and MRV unless explicitly overridden.
2. Recovery Capacity SHALL cap weekly allocation.
3. Weak-point prioritization MAY redistribute, not increase, total weekly volume.
4. Deload weeks SHALL reduce allocation before progression resumes.

## Validation

- volume_profile_id MUST be unique.
- MEV < MAV < MRV.
- Weekly sets MUST be non-negative integers.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Recovery Capacity
- Programming
- Load Prescription
- Periodization
- Weak Point

Provides:
- Workout Execution
- Analytics
- Coaching Explanation

23.name: FATIGUE MANAGEMENT ENGINE
23.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Fatigue Management Engine monitors acute and chronic fatigue accumulation, classifies fatigue sources, and applies deterministic interventions to maintain adaptation while minimizing excessive fatigue.

## Schema

```yaml
engine: fatigue_management
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: fatigue_profile_id

fields:
  fatigue_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  local_fatigue: integer
  systemic_fatigue: integer
  neural_fatigue: integer
  accumulated_fatigue: integer
  fatigue_zone: low|moderate|high|critical
  intervention:
    volume: decrease|maintain|increase
    intensity: decrease|maintain|increase
    recovery_days: integer
```

## Fatigue Zones

| Zone | Score |
|---|---|
| Low | 0-24 |
| Moderate | 25-49 |
| High | 50-74 |
| Critical | 75-100 |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| FM001 | Fatigue >75 | Immediate recovery intervention |
| FM002 | Fatigue 50-74 | Reduce weekly volume |
| FM003 | Fatigue <25 | Progression permitted |
| FM004 | High neural fatigue | Reduce intensity before volume |
| FM005 | High systemic fatigue | Schedule additional recovery |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
fatigue_profile_id: FAT_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
local_fatigue: 48
systemic_fatigue: 37
neural_fatigue: 28
accumulated_fatigue: 41
fatigue_zone: moderate
intervention:
  volume: maintain
  intensity: maintain
  recovery_days: 2
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Fatigue SHALL be evaluated before progression.
2. Critical fatigue SHALL override performance objectives.
3. Recovery Capacity SHALL modify fatigue thresholds.
4. Fatigue scoring SHALL be reproducible for identical inputs.

## Validation

- fatigue_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Fatigue scores MUST remain between 0 and 100.
- Recovery days MUST be non-negative.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Recovery Engine
- Readiness Engine
- Load Prescription Engine
- Volume Allocation Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Analytics

24.name: AUTOREGULATION ENGINE
24.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Autoregulation Engine adjusts daily training prescriptions using objective readiness, fatigue, performance trends, and recovery metrics while preserving the long-term periodization plan.

## Schema

```yaml
engine: autoregulation
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: autoregulation_profile_id

fields:
  autoregulation_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  readiness_score: integer
  fatigue_score: integer
  recovery_score: integer
  planned_load_modifier_percent: float
  planned_volume_modifier_percent: float
  session_decision: proceed|modified|deload|cancel
```

## Decision Matrix

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| AR001 | Readiness ≥85 & Fatigue <30 | Increase load 2–5% |
| AR002 | Readiness 60–84 | Execute planned session |
| AR003 | Readiness 40–59 | Reduce load and/or volume |
| AR004 | Readiness <40 | Replace with recovery session |
| AR005 | Safety flag present | Cancel or substitute session |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
autoregulation_profile_id: AUTO_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
readiness_score: 82
fatigue_score: 34
recovery_score: 80
planned_load_modifier_percent: 0
planned_volume_modifier_percent: 0
session_decision: proceed
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Safety overrides all autoregulation decisions.
2. Autoregulation SHALL modify execution, not long-term goals.
3. Identical inputs SHALL produce identical outputs.
4. Deload recommendations from Periodization take precedence.

## Validation

- autoregulation_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Scores MUST remain between 0 and 100.
- Session decisions MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Readiness Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Fatigue Management Engine
- Load Prescription Engine
- Periodization Engine

Provides:
- Workout Execution
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine

25.name: GOAL OPTIMIZATION ENGINE
25.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Goal Optimization Engine prioritizes training decisions according to the athlete's primary and secondary goals while preserving deterministic safety, recovery, and programming constraints.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: goal_optimization
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: goal_profile_id

fields:
  goal_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  primary_goal: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|fat_loss|general_fitness
  secondary_goal: string|null
  goal_priority_score: integer
  optimization_targets:
    volume: increase|maintain|decrease
    intensity: increase|maintain|decrease
    frequency: increase|maintain|decrease
    exercise_bias: compound|isolation|balanced
  constraints:
    recovery_capacity: integer
    readiness: integer
    medical_flags: list
```

## Canonical Goal Priorities

| Goal | Primary Optimization |
|---|---|
| Strength | Load progression |
| Hypertrophy | Weekly volume |
| Power | Velocity and intent |
| Endurance | Work capacity |
| Fat Loss | Energy expenditure with muscle retention |
| General Fitness | Balanced development |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| GO001 | Safety conflict present | Override optimization |
| GO002 | Recovery Capacity low | Reduce optimization intensity |
| GO003 | Plateau confirmed | Modify strategy |
| GO004 | Secondary goal conflicts | Preserve primary goal |
| GO005 | Readiness <60 | Delay progression |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
goal_profile_id: GOAL_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
primary_goal: hypertrophy
secondary_goal: strength
goal_priority_score: 95

optimization_targets:
  volume: increase
  intensity: maintain
  frequency: maintain
  exercise_bias: balanced

constraints:
  recovery_capacity: 78
  readiness: 82
  medical_flags: []
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Primary goals SHALL override secondary goals.
2. Safety SHALL override optimization.
3. MEV, MAV and MRV SHALL never be violated.
4. Outputs SHALL be deterministic.
5. Every recommendation SHALL remain traceable.

## Validation

- goal_profile_id MUST be unique.
- Goal enums MUST use canonical values.
- Scores MUST be between 0 and 100.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Programming Engine
- Exercise Selection Engine
- Load Prescription Engine
- Volume Allocation Engine
- Fatigue Management Engine
- Autoregulation Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Analytics

26.name: ADAPTATION TRACKING ENGINE
26.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Adaptation Tracking Engine measures long-term physiological and performance adaptations. It compares expected versus observed responses and provides deterministic feedback to progression, periodization, and programming engines.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: adaptation_tracking
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: adaptation_profile_id

fields:
  adaptation_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  adaptation_domain:
    strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|body_composition|skill
  baseline_score: float
  current_score: float
  expected_change_percent: float
  observed_change_percent: float
  adaptation_status:
    below_expected|on_track|above_expected
  confidence_score: integer
```

## Adaptation Domains

| Domain | Primary Metric |
|---|---|
| Strength | Load / 1RM progression |
| Hypertrophy | Muscle size proxy / volume response |
| Power | Velocity & peak power |
| Endurance | Work capacity |
| Body Composition | Lean/Fat mass trend |
| Skill | Technical consistency |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| AT001 | Observed < Expected for 2 mesocycles | Investigate limiting factors |
| AT002 | Above expected | Maintain progression |
| AT003 | Plateau + Below expected | Escalate to Plateau Engine |
| AT004 | Recovery inadequate | Delay adaptation assessment |
| AT005 | Poor adherence | Suspend adaptation conclusions |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
adaptation_profile_id: ADAPT_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
adaptation_domain: strength
baseline_score: 100
current_score: 110
expected_change_percent: 8
observed_change_percent: 10
adaptation_status: above_expected
confidence_score: 93
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Adaptation SHALL be evaluated over validated time windows.
2. Short-term fluctuations SHALL NOT be treated as adaptation.
3. Adherence and recovery SHALL be verified before conclusions.
4. Identical inputs SHALL produce identical outputs.

## Validation

- adaptation_profile_id MUST be unique.
- confidence_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- adaptation_status MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Goal Optimization Engine
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Fatigue Management Engine
- Adherence Engine
- Plateau Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Goal Optimization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Analytics

27.name: PREDICTIVE PROGRESSION ENGINE
27.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Predictive Progression Engine forecasts short and medium-term training outcomes using deterministic trends from performance, recovery, adherence, fatigue, and adaptation. It recommends future progression only when objective evidence supports continued advancement.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: predictive_progression
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: prediction_profile_id

fields:
  prediction_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  prediction_horizon_weeks: integer
  target_metric: strength|hypertrophy|power|endurance|body_composition
  projected_change_percent: float
  confidence_score: integer
  limiting_factor:
    recovery|fatigue|adherence|plateau|skill|none
  recommendation:
    progress|maintain|deload|modify
```

## Prediction Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| PP001 | Adaptation above expected | Continue progression |
| PP002 | Plateau detected | Freeze prediction until resolved |
| PP003 | Recovery Capacity low | Reduce projected gains |
| PP004 | Adherence poor | Lower confidence |
| PP005 | Fatigue critical | Recommend deload |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
prediction_profile_id: PRED_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
prediction_horizon_weeks: 6
target_metric: strength
projected_change_percent: 4.8
confidence_score: 91
limiting_factor: none
recommendation: progress
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Predictions SHALL be derived only from validated engine outputs.
2. Confidence SHALL decrease with missing or inconsistent data.
3. Predictions SHALL never override safety recommendations.
4. Identical inputs SHALL produce identical predictions.

## Validation

- prediction_profile_id MUST be unique.
- confidence_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- prediction_horizon_weeks MUST be greater than zero.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Adaptation Tracking Engine
- Goal Optimization Engine
- Fatigue Management Engine
- Recovery Capacity Engine
- Adherence Engine
- Plateau Engine

Provides:
- Programming Engine
- Periodization Engine
- Coaching Explanation Engine
- Analytics

28.name: DECISION AUDIT ENGINE
28.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Decision Audit Engine records every deterministic decision produced by the fitness reasoning system. It provides full traceability, reproducibility, and explainability by linking recommendations to the exact upstream rules and engine outputs that generated them.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: decision_audit
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: audit_record_id

fields:
  audit_record_id: string
  timestamp: datetime
  athlete_id: string
  decision_type: programming|exercise_selection|load|volume|recovery|progression
  source_engines: list
  source_rule_ids: list
  input_hash: string
  output_hash: string
  confidence_score: integer
  overridden_by: string|null
```

## Audit Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| DA001 | Recommendation generated | Create audit record |
| DA002 | Safety override | Record override source |
| DA003 | Manual modification | Preserve original and modified outputs |
| DA004 | Repeated inputs | Produce identical output hashes |
| DA005 | Missing evidence | Reject audit entry |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
audit_record_id: AUDIT_001
timestamp: 2026-07-09T12:00:00Z
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
decision_type: programming
source_engines:
  - periodization
  - recovery_capacity
  - programming
source_rule_ids:
  - PD003
  - RC002
  - PG004
input_hash: a13b72
output_hash: d98c41
confidence_score: 97
overridden_by: null
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every recommendation SHALL produce exactly one audit record.
2. Audit records SHALL be immutable.
3. Input and output hashes SHALL uniquely identify a decision.
4. Safety overrides SHALL always be logged.
5. Identical inputs SHALL produce identical audit records.

## Validation

- audit_record_id MUST be unique.
- confidence_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- source_engines and source_rule_ids MUST NOT be empty.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- All reasoning engines
- Coaching Explanation Engine

Provides:
- Analytics
- Explainability Layer
- Compliance Reports
- Debugging Tools

29.name: DATA VALIDATION ENGINE
29.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Data Validation Engine verifies the integrity, completeness, consistency, and safety of all incoming athlete, exercise, and programming data before it enters the reasoning pipeline.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: data_validation
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: validation_record_id

fields:
  validation_record_id: string
  dataset_type: athlete|exercise|program|session|recovery
  validation_status: passed|warning|failed
  validation_score: integer
  errors: list
  warnings: list
  corrected_fields: list
```

## Validation Categories

| Category | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Schema | Required fields and types |
| Range | Numeric bounds |
| Referential | Cross-engine references |
| Consistency | Logical relationships |
| Safety | Unsafe values or contradictions |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| DV001 | Missing required field | Fail validation |
| DV002 | Enum outside canonical values | Reject field |
| DV003 | Invalid engine reference | Fail validation |
| DV004 | Numeric value outside limits | Flag warning or fail |
| DV005 | Safety contradiction detected | Block reasoning pipeline |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
validation_record_id: VALID_001
dataset_type: athlete
validation_status: passed
validation_score: 99
errors: []
warnings: []
corrected_fields: []
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Validation SHALL occur before any reasoning engine executes.
2. Failed validation SHALL block downstream processing.
3. Automatic corrections SHALL be logged.
4. Identical datasets SHALL produce identical validation results.

## Validation

- validation_record_id MUST be unique.
- validation_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- validation_status MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- All upstream data sources

Provides:
- All reasoning engines
- Decision Audit Engine
- Analytics

30.name: KNOWLEDGE CONSISTENCY ENGINE
30.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Knowledge Consistency Engine verifies that all reasoning engines, schemas, identifiers, enums, and cross-engine references remain internally consistent. It prevents contradictory logic from entering the deterministic reasoning layer.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: knowledge_consistency
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: consistency_record_id

fields:
  consistency_record_id: string
  check_scope: engine|schema|reference|enum|rule
  status: passed|warning|failed
  conflicting_items: list
  resolution: string|null
  confidence_score: integer
```

## Consistency Checks

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Engine References | Validate referenced engines exist |
| Schemas | Validate required fields and types |
| Enums | Ensure canonical enum values |
| Rules | Detect contradictory deterministic rules |
| IDs | Verify unique identifiers |

## Decision Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| KC001 | Missing engine reference | Fail check |
| KC002 | Duplicate identifier | Reject dataset |
| KC003 | Conflicting deterministic rules | Flag critical error |
| KC004 | Invalid enum value | Reject record |
| KC005 | Circular dependency | Block integration |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
consistency_record_id: CONS_001
check_scope: engine
status: passed
conflicting_items: []
resolution: null
confidence_score: 100
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Consistency checks SHALL run before release.
2. Critical failures SHALL block deployment.
3. All cross-engine references SHALL be validated.
4. Identical repositories SHALL produce identical consistency reports.

## Validation

- consistency_record_id MUST be unique.
- confidence_score MUST be between 0 and 100.
- status MUST use canonical enums.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- All reasoning engines
- Data Validation Engine
- Decision Audit Engine

Provides:
- Release Validation
- Analytics
- Debugging Tools

32.name: KNOWLEDGE VERSIONING ENGINE
32.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Knowledge Versioning Engine manages version control for the Fitness Knowledge Base. It tracks engine revisions, schema changes, rule modifications, compatibility, and deprecation to ensure deterministic reproducibility across releases.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: knowledge_versioning
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: version_record_id

fields:
  version_record_id: string
  engine_id: string
  engine_version: string
  schema_version: string
  release_date: date
  change_type: major|minor|patch
  compatibility:
    backward: boolean
    forward: boolean
  deprecated_rules: list
  replacement_rules: list
  checksum: string
```

## Versioning Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| KV001 | Major schema change | Increment major version |
| KV002 | Backward-compatible enhancement | Increment minor version |
| KV003 | Bug fix | Increment patch version |
| KV004 | Rule deprecated | Record replacement |
| KV005 | Checksum mismatch | Reject deployment |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
version_record_id: VER_001
engine_id: engine_32
engine_version: 7.0.0
schema_version: 1.0.0
release_date: 2026-07-09
change_type: major
compatibility:
  backward: true
  forward: false
deprecated_rules: []
replacement_rules: []
checksum: sha256:abcd1234
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Every engine SHALL have a unique version record.
2. Version history SHALL be immutable.
3. Deprecated rules SHALL reference replacements.
4. Releases SHALL include checksum verification.
5. Identical repositories SHALL produce identical version metadata.

## Validation

- version_record_id MUST be unique.
- engine_version SHALL follow semantic versioning.
- checksum MUST be present.
- compatibility values MUST be boolean.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Knowledge Consistency Engine
- Decision Audit Engine

Provides:
- Release Management
- Deployment Pipeline
- Analytics

33.name: CONFIGURATION MANAGEMENT ENGINE

36.name: SYSTEM GOVERNANCE ENGINE
36.spec_text:

Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The System Governance Engine is the final authority for the Fitness Knowledge Base. It enforces global policies, security, compliance, release approval, audit integrity, and deterministic governance across all engines.

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| SG001 | Compliance failure | Block release |
| SG002 | Security violation | Reject execution |
| SG003 | Missing audit trail | Prevent deployment |
| SG004 | Checksum mismatch | Reject package |
