3. Audit records SHALL be preserved.
4. Approved releases SHALL have immutable checksums.
5. Identical repositories SHALL produce identical governance outcomes.

## Validation

- governance_profile_id MUST be unique.
- compliance_status MUST use canonical enums.
- approved_release MUST be boolean.
- final_checksum MUST be present.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Decision Audit Engine
- Knowledge Versioning Engine
- Configuration Management Engine
- Deployment & Environment Engine
- Monitoring & Observability Engine

Provides:
- Release Approval
- Compliance Reports
- Enterprise Governance
- Final System Validation

37.name: CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT ENGINE
37.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Continuous Improvement Engine evaluates system performance over time, identifies opportunities for knowledge base refinement, validates proposed improvements, and ensures that updates remain deterministic, traceable, and scientifically justified.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: continuous_improvement
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: improvement_record_id

fields:
  improvement_record_id: string
  proposal_id: string
  source: user_feedback|scientific_evidence|audit|performance_metrics
  affected_engines: list
  improvement_type: rule_update|schema_update|content_update|bug_fix
  evidence_level: low|moderate|high
  validation_status: proposed|validated|approved|rejected
  implementation_version: string
```

## Improvement Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| CI001 | New evidence validated | Create improvement proposal |
| CI002 | Conflicts existing rule | Route to Knowledge Consistency |
| CI003 | Validation failed | Reject proposal |
| CI004 | Approved proposal | Schedule next release |
| CI005 | Major rule change | Increment major version |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
improvement_record_id: IMP_001
proposal_id: PROP_001
source: scientific_evidence
affected_engines:
  - engine_05
  - engine_17
improvement_type: rule_update
evidence_level: high
validation_status: approved
implementation_version: 7.1.0
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Improvements SHALL never modify released knowledge directly.
2. Every approved improvement SHALL be versioned.
3. Scientific evidence SHALL be validated before adoption.
4. All changes SHALL remain fully auditable and reproducible.

## Validation

- improvement_record_id MUST be unique.
- validation_status MUST use canonical enums.
- implementation_version SHALL follow semantic versioning.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Decision Audit Engine
- Knowledge Versioning Engine
- Knowledge Consistency Engine
- System Governance Engine

Provides:
- Release Management
- Knowledge Versioning
- Analytics

38.name: RESEARCH INTEGRATION ENGINE
38.spec_text:
Version: 7.0.0
Status: COMPLETE (Foundation)

## Purpose

The Research Integration Engine governs how new scientific literature is evaluated, validated, and incorporated into the Fitness Knowledge Base without compromising determinism, safety, or reproducibility.

---

## Schema

```yaml
engine: research_integration
version: 7.0.0
primary_key: research_record_id

fields:
  research_record_id: string
  publication_id: string
  source_type: systematic_review|meta_analysis|rct|cohort|case_study|expert_consensus
  evidence_grade: A|B|C|D
  affected_engines: list
  integration_status: pending|validated|approved|rejected
  reviewer: string
  implementation_version: string
```

## Integration Rules

| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| RI001 | High-quality evidence validated | Create integration proposal |
| RI002 | Evidence conflicts existing rule | Route to Knowledge Consistency |
| RI003 | Evidence grade below threshold | Reject integration |
| RI004 | Governance approval received | Schedule implementation |
| RI005 | Major rule change | Increment knowledge version |

## Sample Profile

```yaml
research_record_id: RES_001
publication_id: PMID123456
source_type: meta_analysis
evidence_grade: A
affected_engines:
  - engine_05
  - engine_17
integration_status: approved
reviewer: scientific_board
implementation_version: 7.2.0
```

## Deterministic Rules

1. Only validated evidence SHALL modify future releases.
2. Every integration SHALL be traceable to its source publication.
3. Existing releases SHALL remain immutable.
4. All accepted research SHALL undergo governance review.

## Validation

- research_record_id MUST be unique.
- evidence_grade MUST use canonical values.
- integration_status MUST use canonical enums.
- implementation_version SHALL follow semantic versioning.

## Interfaces

Consumes:
- Continuous Improvement Engine
- Knowledge Versioning Engine
- Knowledge Consistency Engine
- System Governance Engine

Provides:
- Release Management
- Continuous Improvement
- Knowledge Versioning

39.name: DIET PHASE ENGINE
39.spec_text:
Version: 1.0.0
Status: NEW (V1 addition — not KB-sourced from prior files; built fresh for this version)

## Purpose
Computes calorie targets, macro splits, and diet-phase classification (bulk / cut / recomp / maintenance),
coordinated with the athlete's primary goal (Engine 25) and training volume (Engine 22). This is the
gap the old `engines/nutrition` module explicitly did NOT cover (it only handles supplement safety).

## Schema
```yaml
engine: diet_phase
version: 1.0.0
primary_key: diet_profile_id

fields:
  diet_profile_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  bodyweight_kg: float
  activity_multiplier: float          # 1.2-1.9 (sedentary to very active)
  phase: bulk|cut|recomp|maintenance
  goal_alignment: string              # references Engine 25 goal_profile_id
  bmr_kcal: integer
  tdee_kcal: integer
  target_kcal: integer
  macro_split:
    protein_g: integer
    carbs_g: integer
    fat_g: integer
  protein_per_kg: float
  phase_duration_weeks: integer
  refeed_frequency: string
```

## Canonical Phase Rules
| Phase | Kcal Adjustment | Protein (g/kg) | Typical Duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| Bulk | TDEE + 10-20% | 1.6-2.2 | 8-16 weeks |
| Cut | TDEE - 15-25% | 2.0-2.6 | 6-12 weeks |
| Recomp | TDEE +/- 5% | 1.8-2.4 | 12-20 weeks |
| Maintenance | TDEE +/- 0% | 1.6-2.0 | Ongoing |

## Decision Rules
| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| DP001 | goal_profile.primary_goal == fat_loss | phase = cut, protein_per_kg >= 2.0 |
| DP002 | goal_profile.primary_goal == hypertrophy AND training_age < 1yr | phase = bulk (novice surplus tolerance) |
| DP003 | goal_profile.primary_goal == hypertrophy AND training_age >= 1yr | phase = recomp (slower gains, less fat cost) |
| DP004 | recovery_capacity (Engine 10) < 50 | cap deficit at 15% max, regardless of requested phase |
| DP005 | medical_flags present (disordered eating history) | route to Constraints Engine; block auto-generated deficit targets |
| DP006 | phase_duration_weeks elapsed | trigger re-assessment, do not auto-continue same phase indefinitely |

## BMR Calculation (Mifflin-St Jeor, sex-specific offset)
```
BMR (male)   = 10*weight_kg + 6.25*height_cm - 5*age + 5
BMR (female) = 10*weight_kg + 6.25*height_cm - 5*age - 161
TDEE = BMR * activity_multiplier
```

## Sample Profiles

```yaml
diet_profile_id: DIET_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
bodyweight_kg: 78
activity_multiplier: 1.55
phase: recomp
goal_alignment: GOAL_001
bmr_kcal: 1780
tdee_kcal: 2759
target_kcal: 2759
macro_split:
  protein_g: 172
  carbs_g: 310
  fat_g: 77
protein_per_kg: 2.2
phase_duration_weeks: 16
refeed_frequency: none
```

```yaml
diet_profile_id: DIET_002
athlete_id: ATHLETE_002
bodyweight_kg: 65
activity_multiplier: 1.4
phase: cut
goal_alignment: GOAL_002
bmr_kcal: 1390
tdee_kcal: 1946
target_kcal: 1557
macro_split:
  protein_g: 156
  carbs_g: 130
  fat_g: 52
protein_per_kg: 2.4
phase_duration_weeks: 10
refeed_frequency: weekly
```

## Validation
- target_kcal MUST NOT fall below 1.0x BMR under any rule (hard safety floor).
- protein_per_kg MUST be within 1.4-2.8 g/kg (outside this range = flag for manual review).
- phase MUST reconcile with goal_alignment; conflicting phase/goal pairs route to Goal Optimization Engine (25) for override resolution per rule GO004.

## Interfaces
Consumes: Goal Optimization Engine (25), Recovery Capacity Engine (10), Constraints Engine
Provides: Coaching Explanation Engine (18), Analytics Engine (39.5/Engine 42), Programming Engine (19)

## Known Limitations (stated up front, not discovered later)
- BMR formula is a population-average estimate (Mifflin-St Jeor), not individually calibrated — real metabolic
  variance of +/-10-15% between individuals with identical stats is normal and not corrected for here.
- No handling yet for medical conditions affecting metabolism (thyroid conditions, PCOS, etc.) — these route
  to Constraints Engine as a flag, not a computed adjustment.

40.name: WARM-UP & LOAD PRESCRIPTION RAMP ENGINE
40.spec_text:
Version: 1.0.0
Status: NEW (V1 addition)

## Purpose
Converts a prescribed working set (e.g. "3x5 @ 80% 1RM") into an actual warm-up ramp and real weight values,
rounded to available plate increments. This closes the gap where Engine 21 (Load Prescription) computes a
percentage but nothing converts that into what actually gets loaded on the bar.

## Schema
```yaml
engine: warmup_ramp
version: 1.0.0
primary_key: ramp_id

fields:
  ramp_id: string
  exercise_id: string             # references Engine 2
  estimated_1rm_kg: float
  working_set_pct: float
  working_weight_kg: float
  bar_weight_kg: float
  smallest_plate_kg: float
  ramp_sets: list                 # list of {pct, reps, weight_kg}
  rounding_strategy: nearest_available|round_down|round_up
```

## Canonical Warm-up Ramp (compound lifts, working set >= 75% 1RM)
| Set | % of Working Weight | Reps |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Bar only / 40% | 8-10 |
| 2 | 55% | 5 |
| 3 | 70% | 3 |
| 4 | 85% | 2 |
| 5 | 95% | 1 |
| Working sets | 100% | as prescribed |

For working sets < 75% 1RM (hypertrophy-rep-range work), a shortened 2-set ramp is used (50%x8, 75%x4).

## Deterministic Rules
1. Ramp SHALL scale to the actual working weight, not a fixed absolute.
2. Final loaded weight SHALL round to the nearest achievable plate combination given `smallest_plate_kg`
   and `bar_weight_kg` — never prescribe an unloadable weight (e.g. 82.3kg on a bar with only 1.25kg plates minimum).
3. Isolation/machine exercises SHALL use a shortened 2-set ramp regardless of intensity (lower systemic joint risk).
4. If estimated_1rm_kg is unavailable, ramp SHALL fall back to RPE-anchored ramp (no numeric %), never guess a 1RM.

## Plate Math (barbell, kg, one side of bar)
```
available_plates_kg = [25, 20, 15, 10, 5, 2.5, 1.25]
target_per_side_kg = (working_weight_kg - bar_weight_kg) / 2
# Greedy round to nearest achievable combination from available_plates_kg
```

## Sample Profiles

```yaml
ramp_id: RAMP_001
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
estimated_1rm_kg: 140
working_set_pct: 80
working_weight_kg: 112
bar_weight_kg: 20
smallest_plate_kg: 1.25
rounding_strategy: nearest_available
ramp_sets:
  - {pct: 40, reps: 8, weight_kg: 45}
  - {pct: 55, reps: 5, weight_kg: 61.5}
  - {pct: 70, reps: 3, weight_kg: 78.5}
  - {pct: 85, reps: 2, weight_kg: 95}
  - {pct: 95, reps: 1, weight_kg: 106.5}
  - {pct: 100, reps: 5, weight_kg: 112}
```

```yaml
ramp_id: RAMP_002
exercise_id: leg_extension
estimated_1rm_kg: null
working_set_pct: null
working_weight_kg: null
bar_weight_kg: 0
smallest_plate_kg: 2.5
rounding_strategy: nearest_available
ramp_sets:
  - {pct: null, reps: 12, weight_kg: null, note: "RPE-anchored: light warm-up set, RPE 4-5"}
  - {pct: null, reps: 8, weight_kg: null, note: "RPE-anchored: approach set, RPE 6-7"}
```

## Validation
- working_weight_kg MUST be achievable with available_plates_kg + bar_weight_kg (no orphan micro-loads).
- ramp_sets MUST be monotonically increasing in weight.
- Isolation/machine exercise_type (per Engine 2) MUST use the 2-set shortened ramp, never the 5-set compound ramp.

## Interfaces
Consumes: Load Prescription Engine (21), Exercise Metadata (2)
Provides: Programming Engine (19), Coaching Explanation Engine (18)

41.name: SUBSTITUTION (EXERCISE CONFLICT) ENGINE — REBUILT
41.spec_text:
Version: 2.0.0
Status: REBUILT (previous version was an unpopulated 2-line stub, 0 tables extracted — see old GAPS.md)

## Purpose
Determines when two exercises conflict (redundant joint stress, contraindicated pairing, equipment collision)
and provides deterministic substitution when an exercise is unavailable, contraindicated, or flagged by
the Constraints Engine.

## Schema
```yaml
engine: substitution
version: 2.0.0
primary_key: substitution_rule_id

fields:
  substitution_rule_id: string
  source_exercise_id: string
  reason: equipment_unavailable|injury_flag|joint_stress_cap|conflict_with_other_exercise|user_dislike
  candidate_substitutes: list       # ordered by preference, references Engine 2 exercise_id
  equivalence_score: integer        # 0-100, how closely substitute matches original's training effect
  conflict_partners: list           # exercise_ids that should not appear same session as source
```

## Canonical Conflict Categories
| Category | Rule | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Redundant primary-mover overload | Two heavy compound lifts targeting the same primary joint pattern back-to-back at high intensity same session | Heavy back squat + heavy leg press same day |
| Contraindicated pairing (injury) | Constraints Engine flags a joint; any exercise sharing that joint_stress_profile primary_joint is excluded | Shoulder flag excludes all vertical_push AND horizontal_push exercises, not just one |
| Equipment collision | Two prescribed exercises require the same single-unit equipment simultaneously in circuit format | Cable exercise A and Cable exercise B in same superset, one cable stack gym |
| Fatigue stacking | Two exercises with STF fatigue_rating >= 8 in the same session for the same muscle group | Heavy hinge + heavy squat same day, novice trainee |

## Deterministic Rules
1. Substitution SHALL preserve movement_id family where possible (squat->squat) before falling back to a different pattern.
2. equivalence_score SHALL be computed from shared primary_muscles + shared movement_id + shared exercise_type,
   not assigned arbitrarily.
3. Any substitute suggested SHALL pass the same safety_gate() check as the original (Constraints Engine authority is absolute).
4. If NO valid substitute exists above equivalence_score 40, the engine SHALL return `no_safe_substitute: true`
   rather than force a poor match — this is a valid, expected output, not an error state.

## Sample Profiles

```yaml
substitution_rule_id: SUB_001
source_exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
reason: injury_flag
candidate_substitutes:
  - {exercise_id: leg_press, equivalence_score: 72}
  - {exercise_id: goblet_squat, equivalence_score: 68}
  - {exercise_id: hack_squat_machine, equivalence_score: 65}
conflict_partners: [barbell_front_squat, hack_squat_machine]
```

```yaml
substitution_rule_id: SUB_002
source_exercise_id: overhead_press
reason: joint_stress_cap
candidate_substitutes:
  - {exercise_id: landmine_shoulder_press, equivalence_score: 74}
  - {exercise_id: machine_shoulder_press, equivalence_score: 70}
  - {exercise_id: cable_shoulder_press, equivalence_score: 66}
conflict_partners: [push_press, z_press, handstand_push_up]
```

```yaml
substitution_rule_id: SUB_003
source_exercise_id: conventional_deadlift
reason: joint_stress_cap
candidate_substitutes:
  - {exercise_id: trap_bar_deadlift, equivalence_score: 78}
  - {exercise_id: dumbbell_rdl, equivalence_score: 60}
  - {exercise_id: cable_pull_through, equivalence_score: 52}
conflict_partners: [good_morning, stiff_leg_deadlift, deficit_deadlift]
```

## Validation
- Every source_exercise_id and candidate_substitutes entry MUST resolve to a real Engine 2 exercise_id.
- equivalence_score MUST be reproducible from the stated formula (muscle overlap + movement_id + type), not hand-waved.
- conflict_partners is symmetric: if A conflicts with B, B's own rule MUST list A.

## Interfaces
Consumes: Exercise Metadata (2), Constraints Engine, Joint Stress Engine (4)
Provides: Exercise Selection Engine (20), Coaching Explanation Engine (18)

## Known Limitation
This engine is now internally consistent and rule-grounded (unlike v1's empty stub), but the equivalence
scores are Claude-estimated from shared tags, not derived from biomechanics literature or coach validation —
treat as a reasonable placeholder ranking, not a scientifically validated equivalence metric.

42.name: ANALYTICS ENGINE — REBUILT
42.spec_text:
Version: 2.0.0
Status: REBUILT (previous version built from 2-line stub sources; this version has fuller worked thresholds)

## Purpose
Computes adherence tracking, plan metadata versioning, and longitudinal trend detection across sessions.

## Schema
```yaml
engine: analytics
version: 2.0.0
primary_key: analytics_record_id

fields:
  analytics_record_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  period_start: date
  period_end: date
  sets_prescribed: integer
  sets_logged: integer
  adherence_pct: float
  adherence_tier: high|moderate|low
  consecutive_high_weeks: integer
  volume_trend: increasing|stable|decreasing
  strength_trend: increasing|stable|decreasing
  kb_version: string
  engine_versions: dict
```

## Canonical Adherence Tiers
| Tier | Threshold | Coaching Action |
|---|---|---|
| High | >= 80% | Continue progression as planned |
| Moderate | 50-79% | Flag for check-in; consider volume reduction |
| Low | < 50% | Trigger re-onboarding / goal reassessment, do not auto-progress |

## Decision Rules
| Rule | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| AN001 | adherence_tier == low for 2+ consecutive periods | Route to Feedback Engine (43) for qualitative check |
| AN002 | consecutive_high_weeks >= 4 | Eligible for progression per Engine 3 |
| AN003 | volume_trend == decreasing AND adherence_tier == high | Signal possible under-recovery, not under-motivation — route to Recovery Capacity Engine (10), not Feedback Engine |
| AN004 | strength_trend == decreasing for 3+ periods despite high adherence | Route to Plateau Engine (11) |

## Sample Profiles

```yaml
analytics_record_id: AN_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
period_start: 2026-06-01
period_end: 2026-06-07
sets_prescribed: 60
sets_logged: 54
adherence_pct: 90.0
adherence_tier: high
consecutive_high_weeks: 5
volume_trend: stable
strength_trend: increasing
kb_version: 7.1.0
engine_versions: {exercise_metadata: 7.1.0, programming: 7.0.0}
```

```yaml
analytics_record_id: AN_002
athlete_id: ATHLETE_003
period_start: 2026-06-01
period_end: 2026-06-07
sets_prescribed: 48
sets_logged: 19
adherence_pct: 39.6
adherence_tier: low
consecutive_high_weeks: 0
volume_trend: decreasing
strength_trend: stable
kb_version: 7.1.0
engine_versions: {exercise_metadata: 7.1.0, programming: 7.0.0}
```

## Validation
- adherence_pct MUST be recomputed from sets_logged/sets_prescribed, never entered independently (prevents drift).
- Tier boundaries are inclusive at the lower bound (exactly 80% = high, exactly 50% = moderate).

## Interfaces
Consumes: Programming Engine (19), Feedback Engine (43)
Provides: Coaching Explanation Engine (18), Plateau Engine (11), Recovery Capacity Engine (10)

## Known Limitation
Thresholds (80%/50% tier cuts, 4-week progression eligibility) are reasonable engineering defaults, not
derived from a validated adherence-outcomes study on your specific user base — expect to tune these once
you have real usage data.

43.name: FEEDBACK / EXPLANATION ENGINE — REBUILT
43.spec_text:
Version: 2.0.0
Status: REBUILT (previous version built from 2-line stub; this version has fuller worked classification logic)

## Purpose
Converts a user's post-workout rating (1-5) and free-text notes into an actionable classification, and
generates plain-language coaching explanations for why a plan decision was made.

## Schema
```yaml
engine: feedback
version: 2.0.0
primary_key: feedback_record_id

fields:
  feedback_record_id: string
  athlete_id: string
  exercise_id: string
  session_date: date
  difficulty_rating: integer        # 1-5
  notes: string|null
  classification: too_easy|appropriate|too_hard|possible_pain_flag|insufficient_data
  pain_keyword_detected: boolean
  routed_to: string|null            # engine this feedback was escalated to, if any
```

## Canonical Classification Rules
| Rule | Condition | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| FB001 | notes contains pain keyword (sharp, twinge, popping, numbness, shooting) | possible_pain_flag | OVERRIDES rating — always checked first |
| FB002 | rating == 1 or 2 AND no pain keyword | too_easy | — |
| FB003 | rating == 4 or 5 AND no pain keyword | too_hard | — |
| FB004 | rating == 3 AND no pain keyword | appropriate | — |
| FB005 | no rating provided, notes present | insufficient_data, but notes still scanned for pain keywords (FB001 still applies) | — |

Note: pain-keyword detection ALWAYS overrides a numeric rating, by design — a 3/5 rating with "felt a sharp
twinge" must never classify as appropriate. This mirrors the KB's own stated design intent for this module.

## Deterministic Rules
1. possible_pain_flag SHALL route to Constraints Engine automatically, not wait for manual coach review.
2. Three consecutive too_easy classifications on the same exercise SHALL trigger a progression suggestion via Engine 3.
3. Three consecutive too_hard classifications SHALL trigger a regression suggestion via Engine 3, not silent continuation.
4. Explanations generated for the athlete SHALL cite the deciding engine/rule in plain language
   (e.g. "reduced today because your recovery capacity score was lower than usual"), never a bare rule ID.

## Sample Profiles

```yaml
feedback_record_id: FB_001
athlete_id: ATHLETE_001
exercise_id: barbell_back_squat
session_date: 2026-06-03
difficulty_rating: 3
notes: "felt good, could probably go heavier next time"
classification: appropriate
pain_keyword_detected: false
routed_to: null
```

```yaml
feedback_record_id: FB_002
athlete_id: ATHLETE_002
exercise_id: overhead_press
session_date: 2026-06-05
difficulty_rating: 3
notes: "sharp pinch in my right shoulder on the last rep"
classification: possible_pain_flag
pain_keyword_detected: true
routed_to: constraints_engine
```

```yaml
feedback_record_id: FB_003
athlete_id: ATHLETE_003
exercise_id: dumbbell_curl
session_date: 2026-06-06
difficulty_rating: 1
notes: null
classification: too_easy
pain_keyword_detected: false
routed_to: null
```

## Validation
- pain_keyword_detected MUST be recomputed from notes text at read time, never cached stale (a later edit to notes must re-trigger detection).
- classification MUST NOT be appropriate or too_easy if pain_keyword_detected is true, under any rating value.

## Interfaces
Consumes: none (leaf input engine, athlete-facing)
Provides: Constraints Engine, Progression & Regression Engine (3), Coaching Explanation Engine (18), Analytics Engine (42)

## Known Limitation
Pain-keyword list is a starting set (sharp, twinge, popping, numbness, shooting) — not exhaustive, and
free-text detection this way will miss non-English phrasing, typos, and indirect descriptions ("it felt wrong").
This is a first-pass safety net, not a substitute for the athlete having a direct way to flag concerns.
